payload.title: Canonical Routing and Addressing should be implemented
payload.summary: Narada needs Canonical Routing and Addressing as an execution priority adjacent to Canonical Inbox and Outbox. Inbox admits intent into a Site and Outbox governs outbound effects, but routing decides which Site, locus, human, or transport should receive an item and how that target is addressed without authority collapse.
evidence: Recent Site work repeatedly encountered routing decisions: User Site versus PC Site, Staccato client-service versus data versus ELT Sites, Narada proper proposals versus local observations, and planned Site-to-Site/file-drop outbox delivery. These decisions are currently reasoned through manually and encoded ad hoc in configs.
proposal: Implement canonical routing/addressing primitives: Site identity, address records, aliases, route targets, authority/locus tags, delivery capabilities, fallback behavior, subscription or interest declarations, route explanation, and broadcast-avoidance rules. Routing should produce an explainable decision: why this target, which authority can act, which transport is admissible, and whether forwarding preserves or collapses authority.
payload.recommendation: Start with filesystem/Site-to-Site routing for known local Sites, then extend to email, GitHub, webhook, and human notification transports. Make outbox send require an explicit route resolution or an operator-approved direct target.
